packs.name: Default Business Builder
packs.description: Standard 11-step sequence for generating and validating new business ideas.

prompts.title: Initial Setup Interview
prompts.prompt:
You are an expert business consultant. Your goal is to extract the user's constraints, skills, and goals to create a Build Spec.

Analyze the user's input and extract the following information:
1. Technical and non-technical skills
2. Time available per week
3. Initial budget
4. Risk tolerance (low, medium, high)
5. Preferred business model (subscription, one-time, hybrid, freemium)
6. Target income and timeline

Format your response as a JSON object matching the BuildConstraints schema.

prompts.title: Generate Business Concepts
prompts.prompt:
Based on the Build Spec constraints, generate 3 highly optimized business concepts.

For each concept, provide:
1. Title and one-sentence description
2. Ideal Customer Profile (ICP)
3. Core pain point solved
4. Measurable outcome for the customer
5. Why this fits the user's constraints (low-touch rationale)
6. Competitive moat
7. Proposed pricing ladder
8. Primary acquisition channels

Format your response as a JSON array of BusinessConcept objects.

prompts.title: Validation Plan
prompts.prompt:
Create a lean, 7-day validation plan for the selected business concept.

The plan must include:
1. The core hypothesis to test
2. The minimum viable offer (MVO)
3. 3 specific, low-cost experiments to run (e.g., cold email, landing page + ads, community posting)
4. Success metrics for each experiment (e.g., 5% conversion rate, 3 pre-sales)
5. A daily schedule of actions

Output the plan in clear markdown format.

prompts.title: Competitive Positioning
prompts.prompt:
Develop a competitive positioning strategy for the selected concept.

1. Identify 3-5 indirect or direct competitors.
2. Map them on a 2x2 matrix (e.g., Price vs. Complexity).
3. Define the 'Positioning Wedge' - the specific angle or niche where this business will dominate.
4. Write a 1-sentence positioning statement: 'For [Target Audience] who [Pain Point], [Product Name] is a [Category] that provides [Core Benefit] unlike [Competitor].'

Output the strategy in markdown format.

prompts.title: Design Core Offer
prompts.prompt:
Design an irresistible core offer that maximizes perceived value while minimizing fulfillment cost.

Include:
1. The Core Promise (Specific outcome + Timeframe)
2. The Unique Mechanism (Why it works when other solutions failed)
3. Deliverables (What exactly they get)
4. Risk Reversal (Guarantee)
5. Scarcity/Urgency elements
6. Pricing Tiers (Basic, Pro, Enterprise)

Format as a structured markdown document.

prompts.title: Brand Identity
prompts.prompt:
Create a cohesive brand identity kit.

Provide:
1. 5 potential brand names (available as .com or .co)
2. A memorable tagline (under 6 words)
3. Brand Voice guidelines (e.g., Professional but witty, Direct and authoritative)
4. Color palette suggestions (Primary, Secondary, Accent with Hex codes)
5. Typography pairings (Header font + Body font)

Output as a markdown document.

prompts.title: Website Copy
prompts.prompt:
Write high-converting copy for a single-page landing site.

Structure the copy using the following framework:
1. Hero Section (H1, H2, Primary CTA)
2. Problem Agitation (The 'Villain')
3. Solution Introduction (The 'Hero')
4. How It Works (3 simple steps)
5. Benefits/Features (Focus on outcomes)
6. Social Proof/Authority markers
7. Pricing Section
8. Final CTA and FAQ

Output the copy in markdown, clearly labeling each section.

prompts.title: Product Blueprint
prompts.prompt:
Create a technical and operational blueprint for the MVP (Minimum Viable Product).

Define:
1. Core User Flow (Step-by-step journey from signup to first value)
2. Required Features (Must-haves for V1)
3. Deferred Features (Nice-to-haves for V2)
4. Recommended Tech Stack (No-code/Low-code preferred based on constraints)
5. Data Models (Core entities and relationships)

Output as a structured markdown document.

prompts.title: Automation Architecture
prompts.prompt:
Design a zero-touch automation architecture for fulfillment and operations.

Map out the following workflows:
1. Customer Onboarding (Payment -> Welcome Email -> Provisioning)
2. Service Fulfillment (How the core value is delivered automatically)
3. Customer Support (Self-serve docs -> AI Bot -> Escalation)
4. Churn Prevention (Failed payment recovery, usage drop-off alerts)

Recommend specific tools (e.g., Zapier, Make, Stripe, Resend) for each workflow.

prompts.title: Go-to-Market Plan
prompts.prompt:
Develop a focused 60-day Go-to-Market (GTM) execution plan.

1. Select ONE primary acquisition channel based on the ICP and constraints.
2. Define the content/outbound strategy for that channel.
3. Create a week-by-week execution timeline (Weeks 1-8).
4. Define the Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) to track weekly.
5. Outline a small budget allocation plan (if applicable).

Output as a markdown document.

packs.name: SaaS Vertical (LaunchOps)
packs.description: 11-step automated launch sequence for SaaS businesses.

prompts.title: Security & Credentials Setup
prompts.prompt:
You are the Orchestrator Agent. Your task is to generate secure credentials and API keys for the new SaaS project.

1. Generate a secure master password.
2. Define the required environment variables for a standard Next.js/Supabase/Stripe stack.
3. Create a plan for storing these securely (e.g., Google Secret Manager or Vercel Env).

Output the plan in markdown. Do NOT output actual raw secrets in plain text, use placeholders.

prompts.title: Legal Formation
prompts.prompt:
You are the Paperwork Agent. Your task is to prepare the legal foundation for the SaaS.

1. Draft the structure for a standard Delaware LLC Operating Agreement.
2. Generate a standard SaaS Terms of Service outline.
3. Generate a standard Privacy Policy outline (GDPR/CCPA compliant).

Output the outlines in markdown format.

prompts.title: Repository Setup
prompts.prompt:
You are the WebDev Agent. Your task is to define the repository structure and CI/CD pipeline.

1. Outline the folder structure for a Next.js App Router project.
2. Define the necessary GitHub Actions workflows (Lint, Test, Build).
3. List the core dependencies to be included in package.json.

Output the structure and configuration in markdown.

prompts.title: Stripe Configuration
prompts.prompt:
You are the Stripe Agent. Your task is to design the billing architecture.

Based on the provided pricing tiers:
1. Define the Stripe Product and Price objects required.
2. Outline the webhook events that need to be handled (e.g., checkout.session.completed, invoice.payment_succeeded).
3. Draft the logic for handling subscription upgrades/downgrades.

Output the architecture in markdown.

prompts.title: Transactional Email Setup
prompts.prompt:
You are the Marketing Agent. Your task is to design the transactional email flows.

Draft the subject lines and core copy for the following essential emails:
1. Welcome / Email Verification
2. Password Reset
3. Subscription Confirmed / Receipt
4. Payment Failed / Dunning
5. Trial Expiring Soon

Output the email templates in markdown.

prompts.title: Website Deployment
prompts.prompt:
You are the WebDev Agent. Your task is to plan the deployment architecture.

1. Define the Vercel deployment configuration.
2. Outline the database schema (e.g., Supabase/PostgreSQL) for Users, Subscriptions, and core app data.
3. Detail the authentication flow (e.g., NextAuth or Supabase Auth).

Output the technical architecture in markdown.

prompts.title: Marketing Automation
prompts.prompt:
You are the Marketing Agent. Your task is to design the marketing automation funnel.

1. Design a 5-day email onboarding sequence to activate new users.
2. Define the trigger events for each email.
3. Outline a lead magnet strategy to capture emails before signup.

Output the sequence and strategy in markdown.

prompts.title: Support Infrastructure
prompts.prompt:
You are the Orchestrator Agent. Your task is to design the customer support system.

1. Outline the structure for a self-serve Help Center / Knowledge Base (Top 5 categories).
2. Define the support escalation path (Bot -> Email -> Human).
3. Draft 3 canned responses for common SaaS queries (Refund request, Bug report, Feature request).

Output the support plan in markdown.

prompts.title: Analytics Implementation
prompts.prompt:
You are the WebDev Agent. Your task is to define the analytics tracking plan.

1. List the core custom events to track (e.g., 'Signed Up', 'Started Trial', 'Used Core Feature').
2. Define the properties to send with each event.
3. Recommend a privacy-friendly analytics stack (e.g., PostHog, Plausible, or Fathom).

Output the tracking plan in markdown.

prompts.title: Project Management
prompts.prompt:
You are the Orchestrator Agent. Your task is to set up the ongoing project management structure.

1. Define the columns for a Kanban board (e.g., Backlog, Up Next, In Progress, Review, Done).
2. Create 5 initial epic/milestone tickets for the first month post-launch.
3. Define the bug reporting template.

Output the project structure in markdown.

prompts.title: Growth & GTM
prompts.prompt:
You are the Business Builder Agent. Your task is to define the Product-Led Growth (PLG) strategy.

1. Identify the 'Aha! Moment' in the product.
2. Design a viral loop or referral mechanism built into the product.
3. Outline a strategy for launching on Product Hunt / Hacker News.

Output the growth strategy in markdown.
